content.thinking: The user is asking me to add a term tracker to an obsoleted term. Let me first get the full context of the issue to understand what exactly needs to be done.

content.content: {"body":" GO:0030641 regulation of cellular pH is defined as Any process involved in the maintenance of an internal equilibrium of hydrogen ions (protons) within a cell or between a cell and its external environment. which sounds more like "ph homeostasis" should the term labels switch ? (see synonyms)","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws6TJCDm","author":{"login":"cmungall"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Maybe we can simplify this: I excluded regulation edges to make it more tractable but if we include we see 2nd order regulation...: ","createdAt":"2024-11-11T16:49:18Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-2468618470","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws6TJdIQ","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Fixed LD for [regulation of intracellular pH](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0051453) -intersection_of: regulates_characteristic OBA:0000050 >> obsolete +intersection_of: regulates_characteristic OBA:0000058 ! cellular pH","createdAt":"2024-11-11T17:50:46Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-2468729360","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws6UYhAe","author":{"login":"smoe"},"authorAssociation":"NONE","body":"Hello, homeostasis I understand as the goal of the regulation, like an ideal state, not the regulation as a process to achieve it.","createdAt":"2024-11-20T20:10:34Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-2489454622","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws6UY6LT","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"In GO, "homeostasis" is a shorthand for homeostatic process GO:0042592 Any biological process involved in the maintenance of an internal steady state.","createdAt":"2024-11-20T21:16:37Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-2489557715","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws6UZPo3","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"To simplify, I think it's necessary to 1. clarify what the difference is, if any, between "regulation of X" from "homeostatic regulation (homeostasis) of X". 2. decide if it makes sense to have child processes like elevation, reduction, reneuralization, which can be separately regulated.","createdAt":"2024-11-20T22:14:44Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-2489645623","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws6U7gJf","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Discussion on the ontology editors call: - [ ] Merge (if annotations are consistent with the merge): 'intracellular pH elevation' 'regulation of intracellular pH' 'regulation of cellular pH' 'regulation of pH' - [ ] Obsolete: 'pH elevation' 'pH reduction' - [ ] Create grouping classes for organelle pH elevation and acidification","createdAt":"2024-11-25T17:29:32Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-2498626143","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7fEITZ","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"We have 3 sets of terms: - regulation of pH GO:0006885 102 - regulation of cellular pH GO:0030641 34 - regulation of intracellular pH GO:0051453 157 - [ ] Obsolete regulation of pH and regulation of cellular pH , with replace by regulation of intracellular pH? I think this is what is intended. We don't have any regulation of non-cellular pH. (I don't know how/whether anyone has captured regulation of blood pH or other body fluids). ---- - pH elevation GO:0045852 3 - intracellular pH elevation GO:0051454 58 - negative regulation of cellular pH reduction | GO:0032848 | 7 - positive regulation of cellular pH reduction GO:0032849 12 - [ ] obsolete pH elevation, negative regulation of cellular pH reduction and positive regulation of cellular pH reduction & replace by intracellular pH elevation ---- - pH reduction GO:0045851 41 - intracellular pH reduction GO:0051452 38 - regulation of cellular pH reduction GO:0032847 0 - [ ] obsolete pH reduction and regulation of cellular pH reduction & replace by intracellular pH reduction Thanks, Pascale","createdAt":"2026-01-13T07:13:05Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-3742401753","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7fETa8","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"There are also the Golgi terms that are quite similar: - Golgi lumen acidification GO:0061795 11 >> OK - positive regulation of Golgi lumen acidification GO:1905528 4 >> obsolete and replace by Golgi lumen acidification - negative regulation of Golgi lumen acidification GO:1905527 0 >> obsolete with no replacement - regulation of Golgi lumen acidification GO:1905526 8 >> obsolete with consider Golgi lumen acidification (since direction is unspecified) ","createdAt":"2026-01-13T07:18:58Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-3742447292","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7fEVng","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Finally: regulation of lysosomal lumen pH GO:0035751 30 obsolete with consider lysosomal lumen acidification GO:0007042 lysosomal lumen pH elevation GO:0035752 (since direction is unspecified) Thanks, Pascale","createdAt":"2026-01-13T07:20:34Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-3742456288","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7fVhwU","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"PMID:15300855 CA2 gene mutation causes renal tubular acidosis (fails to remove acid from the blood). pH is regulated for blood and cerebrospinal fluid. Do we want to only keep intracellular?","createdAt":"2026-01-13T23:08:22Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-3746962452","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7fu-BY","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"> Do we want to only keep intracellular? For now, yes. If needed, we can create 'blood pH homeostasis' and 'cerebrospinal fluid homeostasis' under [multicellular organismal-level homeostasis](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0048871) But we want to keep the split of cellular versus multicellular organismal-level as high as we can. Thanks, Pascale","createdAt":"2026-01-15T08:57:24Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-3753631832","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7jAO3Q","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"GO:0030641 regulation of cellular pH and GO:0051453 regulation of intracellular pH appear to be of the same meaning. Also GO:0051453 is the only child term of GO:0030641. Thus, they should be 'merged'. We will obsolete GO:0030641 and replace it with GO:0051453 regulation of intracellular pH. ","createdAt":"2026-01-28T01:45:18Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-3808488912","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7jCgsD","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"> [ ] Obsolete: > 'pH elevation' > 'pH reduction' PMID:17409310 slc4a1 (AE1) is primarily involved in SYSTEMIC pH regulation, though it also plays a role in intracellular pH regulation as a Cl⁻/HCO₃⁻ exchanger. Thus AE1 is involved in pH elevation not just intracellular pH elevation or reduction. PMID:7762614 "The H(+)-K(+)-ATPase of the gastric parietal cells is responsible for the acidification of the stomach lumen." Also not intracellular per se here. Thus these two terms may not be ready to be obsoleted.","createdAt":"2026-01-28T05:32:31Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-3809086211","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7jCyYC","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"GO:0032848 negative regulation of cellular pH reduction -> replaced by GO:0051454 intracellular pH elevation GO:0032849 positive regulation of cellular pH reduction -> replaced by GO:0051452 intracellular pH reduction GO:0032847 regulation of cellular pH reduction -> consider GO:0051454 intracellular pH elevation GO:0051452 intracellular pH reduction","createdAt":"2026-01-28T05:56:48Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"THUMBS_UP","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-3809158658","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7kv1Gr","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please make a pull request to obsolete 3 terms: GO:0032848 negative regulation of cellular pH reduction -> replaced by GO:0051454 intracellular pH elevation GO:0032849 positive regulation of cellular pH reduction -> replaced by GO:0051452 intracellular pH reduction GO:0032847 regulation of cellular pH reduction -> consider GO:0051454 intracellular pH elevation GO:0051452 intracellular pH reduction The reason for obsoletion is that the term is regulation of regulation, redundant with regulation. Do not need to consider other comments in this ticket.","createdAt":"2026-02-02T23:10:06Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"EYES","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29164#issuecomment-3837743531","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7kv2ke","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"🤖 Working on it... content.content:
{"body":"## Summary

Merges GO:0030641 'regulation of cellular pH' into GO:0051453 'regulation of intracellular pH' as requested.

The terms represent the same biological concept - "cellular pH" and "intracellular pH" are equivalent. GO:0051453 is the more appropriate term to retain as it has a proper logical definition.

## Changes

### GO:0030641 (obsoleted)
- Name prefixed with "obsolete"
- Definition prefixed with "OBSOLETE."
- Synonyms removed (transferred to replacement term)
- is_a relationships removed
- Added comment explaining reason for obsoletion
- Added `is_obsolete: true`
- Added `replaced_by: GO:0051453`

### GO:0051453 (replacement term)
- Now directly inherits from GO:0006885 (regulation of pH) and GO:0030003 (intracellular monoatomic cation homeostasis)
- Synonyms transferred from GO:0030641:
  - "cellular hydrogen ion homeostasis"
  - "proton homeostasis"
- Added new synonym: "regulation of cellular pH" (the obsoleted term's name)

## Checklist

- [x] PLAN: Analyzed obsoletion request - terms are equivalent
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION: Ontology validated before changes
- [x] TERM-SEARCH: Confirmed GO:0051453 is the only child of GO:0030641
- [x] EDITS: Used proper checkout/checkin procedure
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS: GO:0051453 rewired to inherit directly from GO:0030641's parents
- [x] METADATA: Proper obsoletion metadata applied (is_obsolete, replaced_by, comment, namespace preserved)
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION: SPARQL QC checks pass (0 violations)
- [x] REASONING: ELK reasoner passes with no errors

## Note on Annotations

Annotations to GO:0030641 will need to be migrated to GO:0051453. Since `replaced_by` is set, annotation pipelines should handle this automatically.

Fixes: #29164

[Term]
id: GO:0030641
name: obsolete regulation of cellular pH
namespace: biological_process
def: "OBSOLETE. Any process involved in the maintenance of an internal equilibrium of hydrogen ions (protons) within a cell or between a cell and its external environment." [GOC:dph, GOC:mah, GOC:tb]
comment: The reason for obsoletion is that this term is equivalent to 'regulation of intracellular pH' (GO:0051453). The terms 'cellular pH' and 'intracellular pH' refer to the same concept, and GO:0051453 is the more appropriate term with a proper logical definition.
is_obsolete: true
replaced_by: GO:0051453
